title: Nutrition: Calories and Macronutrients
content:
CALORIES

Calorie (kilocalorie/K Calorie): Heat needed to raise 1 kilogram water 1°C.

Values per gram:
- Protein: 4 calories
- Carbohydrate: 4 calories
- Fat: 9 calories

Daily intake distribution (approximate):
- Minimum 15% protein
- Under 25% fat
- About 60% carbohydrate

Daily calorie needs vary by age, weight, activity:
- Adolescent boys, very active men under 55: ~3000 calories
- Women 18-35: ~2000 calories
- Women over 55: ~1700 calories
- Children 1-6: 1100-1600 calories

Activity factor: Multiply weight by 14 calories for basal need. Multiply by activity factor (20% sedentary, 30% outdoor work, 50% heavy labor) and add to basal needs.

PROTEINS

Components: 22 amino acids (14 can be synthesized by body; 8 essential must be present in food).

Key amino acids (most scarce): Isoleucine, lysine, methionine, tryptophan.

Complete proteins (animal sources): Eggs, milk, meat, fish. High total protein; rich in essential amino acids; more utilizable.

Incomplete proteins (vegetable sources): Whole grains, nuts, seeds, legumes (except soybeans). Lower total protein; deficient in one or more essential amino acids; less utilizable.

Complementary proteins: Combine incomplete proteins to improve utilization (grains + legumes). Adding ⅓ complete protein to incomplete combinations brings total to acceptable levels.

Protein Quality Comparison (Biologic Value):
- Eggs: 94
- Milk: 85
- Brown rice: 75
- White rice: 65
- Whole wheat bread: 67
- White bread: 52
- Peanut butter: 43

Daily protein requirement: Approximately 0.4 gram per pound body weight for adults; 1 gram per pound for children 1-3 years. Increase to 0.5 gram per pound for vegetarian diets without animal products.
